title: Sune's Airport Chaos
description: Test your understanding of the video and practise useful airport vocabulary, phrasal verbs and action verbs.

questions.question: What does “drag” mean in the video?
options: To carry something in your arms
To pull something along the ground
questions.answer: To pull something along the ground
questions.explanation: Sune drags his suitcase because he pulls it along the airport floor.

questions.question: What is “check-in” at an airport?
options: The place where you buy food
The place where you register for your flight and leave your suitcase
The place where you sit on the plane
The place where you read a book
questions.answer: The place where you register for your flight and leave your suitcase
questions.explanation: At check-in, passengers confirm their flight and usually put their suitcase on the scale.

questions.question: What is a “scale” used for?
questions.explanation: Sune puts his suitcase on the scale to check how heavy it is.

questions.question: What does “overweight” mean?
questions.explanation: Sune's suitcase is overweight, which means it is heavier than allowed.

questions.question: What are “belongings”?
options: Personal things you own or carry
questions.answer: Personal things you own or carry
questions.explanation: At security, Sune puts his belongings on the tray.

questions.question: What does “go off” mean in this video?
options: To start beeping or making noise suddenly
questions.answer: To start beeping or making noise suddenly
questions.explanation: The metal detector goes off when it starts beeping.

questions.question: What does “bolt” mean?
options: To run suddenly and very fast
questions.answer: To run suddenly and very fast
questions.explanation: Sune bolts toward the gate because his flight is boarding.

questions.question: What does “squeeze through” mean?
options: To fit through a tight space with difficulty
To look at a screen carefully
questions.answer: To fit through a tight space with difficulty
questions.explanation: Sune squeezes through the gate just as it is closing.

questions.question: What does “worn out” mean?
questions.explanation: Sune is worn out after rushing through the airport.

sections.title: Video comprehension
sections.instructions: Answer the questions about the story.

questions.question: Where does Sune arrive at the beginning of the video?
questions.explanation: At the beginning, Sune arrives at the airport with his suitcase.

questions.question: What problem does Sune have at check-in?
questions.explanation: Sune puts his suitcase on the scale and sees that it is overweight.

questions.question: What does Sune try to do with his suitcase?
options: He gives it to another passenger
He hides it under the scale
questions.explanation: Sune tries sitting on the suitcase to push the weight down, but it does not work.

questions.question: Why does the metal detector go off?
questions.explanation: The detector beeps because Sune forgot his belt.

questions.question: What does Sune realize after checking the departures board?
options: He is at the wrong airport
questions.explanation: Sune realizes that his flight is boarding, so he bolts toward the gate.

sections.title: Complete the sentence
sections.instructions: Choose the correct word or expression.

questions.question: At security, please put your ______ on the tray.
questions.explanation: Belongings are the personal things you carry with you.

questions.question: When Sune saw that his flight was boarding, he ______ toward the gate.
questions.explanation: To bolt means to run suddenly and very fast.

questions.question: After all that airport chaos, Sune was completely ______.
questions.explanation: To be worn out means to be extremely tired.
